title: How does Hibernate's caching work, and what are the differences between the first-level cache and second-level cache?

answers.junior: Hibernate uses two types of cache: first-level (session) and second-level (shared across sessions). First-level cache is always enabled.
answers.middle: First-level cache stores entities within a session, second-level cache is shared and configurable. Second-level cache improves performance by reducing database hits.
answers.senior: First-level cache is session-scoped and cannot be disabled. Second-level cache is configurable, supports various providers, and is useful for read-heavy applications. Proper configuration is key for performance.
answers.tricky: Improper cache configuration can cause stale data or memory issues. Second-level cache must be carefully managed in clustered environments. Monitor cache statistics and eviction policies for optimal performance.
